# Agent skills for knowledge and session management (2026-03-14 10:39Z)

_**User**_

你是SK搜索助手。请在以下平台搜索两类技能：

## 搜索目标A：知识库管理
找到支持"Obsidian vault同步、知识图谱、实体关系、PKM"的agent skills。

## 搜索目标B：会话管理/上下文接续
找到支持"跨窗口交接、上下文压缩、长期记忆、session handoff"的agent skills。

## 搜索方法

### 知识库管理 - WebSearch搜索
- "clawhub obsidian knowledge graph skill"
- "clawhub knowledge base management PKM"
- "skills.sh obsidian vault knowledge graph"
- "github SKILL.md obsidian knowledge graph entity"
- "github agent-skills knowledge management PKM"
- "cursor agent skills obsidian ontology 2026"

### 会话管理 - WebSearch搜索
- "clawhub session management context handoff"
- "clawhub long term memory context compression"
- "skills.sh session handoff context"
- "github SKILL.md session context handoff memory"
- "github agent-skills context compression long-term memory"
- "cursor agent skills session management memory 2026"

## 输出要求
对每个类别的每个找到的候选SK，列出：
- 名称
- 来源
- 描述
- 星数/下载量
- 最后更新
- 安装方式
- URL

每类尽量找3个以上候选。明确区分两个类别的结果。

_**Agent (model claude-4.6-opus-high, mode Agent)**_

[2026-02-10-clawhub-clawvault-1-5-1 skill by openclaw/skills](https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/2026-02-10-clawhub-clawvault-1-5-1)<br/><br/># ClawHub, Obsidian, and Knowledge Graph Skills for Agents<br/><br/>**ClawHub** is the public skill registry for OpenClaw, a platform for publishing, versioning, and searching text-based agent skills.[3] It features a CLI-friendly API, vector search, and moderation capabilities.<br/><br/>## Obsidian Skill<br/><br/>The **Obsidian skill** integrates Obsidian vaults (markdown-based note folders) into AI agent workflows.[1][2] Key features include:<br/><br/>- Fast fuzzy/phonetic search across notes (ripgrep-backed)<br/>- Create, read, and edit notes with frontmatter support<br/>- Tag and wikilink management<br/>- Auto-folder detection for organizing new notes<br/>- JSON output for automation and integration[1]<br/><br/>Install via: `npx clawhub@latest install obsidian`[2]<br/><br/>## Knowledge Graph & Memory Skills<br/><br/>**ClawVault** is a structured memory system for agents that builds a memory graph with context-awareness.[4][5] It includes:<br/><br/>- Memory graph indexing and semantic search<br/>- Context death resilience through checkpoint/recovery<br/>- Obsidian integration with canvas dashboards and task tracking<br/>- Session transcript repair capabilities<br/>- Optional Tailscale networking for cross-vault search[4]<br/><br/>Both skills enable agents to maintain persistent, searchable knowledge bases—Obsidian for general note management and ClawVault for agent-specific memory persistence with graph-aware context retrieval. |

</details>
</tool-use>

[knowledge-management skill by openclaw/skills](https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/knowledge-management)<br/>4. [openclaw/clawhub](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub)<br/>5. [Skills & ClawHub — Extending OpenClaw – Learn OpenClaw](https://learnopenclaw.com/core-concepts/skills)<br/><br/># ClawHub Knowledge Base Management for PKM Skills<br/><br/>## What is ClawHub?<br/><br/>ClawHub is a public skill registry and marketplace for OpenClaw AI agents.[1] It hosts over 3,286 community-built skills as of 2026, with more than 1.5 million total downloads.[1] ClawHub serves as the central hub for discovering, installing, and managing extensions that give OpenClaw agents new capabilities.[2]<br/><br/>## Knowledge Management Skill<br/><br/>ClawHub includes a dedicated **knowledge-management skill** designed for personal knowledge management (PKM).[3] This skill:<br/><br/>- Organizes OpenClaw knowledge entries into a structured local knowledge base<br/>- Automatically classifies entries by content type (Research, Decision, Insight, Lesson, Pattern, Project, Reference, Tutorial)<br/>- Saves timestamped markdown files organized in appropriate folders<br/>- Requires no API keys—it works entirely locally<br/><br/>### Core Commands[3]<br/>- `km sync` – Sync memory entries to local files<br/>- `km classify` – Parse and classify entries without storing (JSON output)<br/>- `km summarize` – Generate index files for each content type<br/>- `km cleanup` – Remove orphaned files<br/><br/>## How Skills Work<br/><br/>Skills are self-contained extensions that add capabilities to OpenClaw agents.[5] Each skill can be installed, updated, and removed independently through simple commands like `/skills install @author/skill-name`.[5] Skills are stored in your workspace's `skills/` directory and configured in your `openclaw.json` file.[5]<br/><br/>## Security Considerations<br/><br/>As of 2026, ClawHub users should be aware of security risks. The "ClawHavoc" incident in February 2026 exposed 341 malicious skills designed to exfiltrate data.[1][2] Modern skills use SHA-256 signature verification to prevent tampering during installation.[2] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[adriangrantdotorg/Obsidian-Skills](https://github.com/adriangrantdotorg/Obsidian-SKILLS)<br/><br/># Skills.sh, Obsidian Vault Knowledge Graph, and AI Agent Skills<br/><br/>**Skills.sh** is an open-source ecosystem for reusable AI agent capabilities.[1] Skills are installable extensions that provide procedural knowledge to enhance agent functionality, installed via `npx skills add`.[1][3] The platform features a leaderboard of 88,000+ skills ranked by installation popularity.[1]<br/><br/>**How Skills Work**: Skills are directories containing a `SKILL.md` file with metadata and instructions, plus optional assets, docs, and scripts.[2] Agents use "progressive disclosure," reviewing skill frontmatter first and only accessing full details when relevant to the task.[2]<br/><br/>**Obsidian Knowledge Graph Integration**: The `obsidian-graph-query` skill enables AI agents to query an Obsidian vault's knowledge graph directly.[4] It uses graph algorithms (BFS, shortest path, bridge detection, degree analysis) to answer questions like "Which notes have the most links?" and "What are isolated notes?"[4] Installation is one command: `npx skills add azuma520/obsidian-graph-query`.[4]<br/><br/>**Obsidian Agent Skills**: A separate collection of skills covers Obsidian best practices for plugin development, vault management, and CLI workflows.[5] These skills are compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and other AI assistants following the Agent Skills standard.[5]<br/><br/>All skills follow the [Agent Skills Specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) with constraints like a 10 MB file size limit for Deep Agents.[2] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[agent-context skill by openclaw/skills](https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/agent-context)<br/><br/># ClawHub Session Management & Context Handoff<br/><br/>ClawHub is the public skill registry for OpenClaw, a platform that manages AI agent capabilities through versioned skill bundles.[3][4]<br/><br/>## Session Handoff Skill<br/><br/>The **Session Handoff** skill solves the context exhaustion problem by creating comprehensive handoff documents that enable fresh AI agents to seamlessly continue work.[1] It triggers when:<br/>- Users request to save state or create handoff<br/>- Context window approaches capacity<br/>- Major task milestones complete<br/>- Work sessions end<br/>- Resuming from existing handoffs<br/><br/>Install via: `npx clawhub@latest install session-handoff`[1]<br/><br/>## Agent-to-Agent Communication<br/><br/>The **Claw-to-Claw (C2C)** skill enables AI agents to coordinate with each other on behalf of users, allowing agents to plan, schedule, and exchange messages while maintaining human approval gates.[2]<br/><br/>## Context Management<br/><br/>The **Agent Context** skill provides persistent project context across sessions using a two-file system:[5]<br/>- **AGENTS.md** — Shared project knowledge (kept under 120 lines)<br/>- **.agents.local.md** — Personal session notes (gitignored)<br/><br/>This enables seamless access to shared knowledge and personal learnings across agent sessions without requiring agents to request information manually.[5]<br/><br/>## ClawHub Registry<br/><br/>ClawHub itself manages skill discovery, versioning, and installation through a CLI-friendly API with vector search, moderation tools, and telemetry tracking.[3][4] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[claw-compactor \| Skills Marketplace · LobeHub](https://lobehub.com/skills/openclaw-skills-cut-your-tokens-97percent-savings-on-session-transcripts-via-observation-extraction)<br/>2. [Context Compressor Skill - Automated Context Management \| SkillKit \| SkillKit](https://skillkit.io/skills/openclaw/context-compressor-skill)<br/>3. [openclaw/clawhub](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub)<br/>4. [Neural Memory — AI Agent Skill \| Install, Stats & Docs \| ClawHub Skills Lib](https://clawhub-skills.com/skills/neural-memory)<br/>5. [Github](https://lobehub.com/pl/skills/openclaw-skills-better-memory)<br/><br/># ClawHub Long-Term Memory & Context Compression Skills<br/><br/>ClawHub offers several skills for managing long-term memory and compressing context in AI agent sessions:<br/><br/>## Key Skills<br/><br/>**Claw Compactor v6.0** [1] delivers 50%+ storage and bandwidth savings through rule-based compression, dictionary encoding, and progressive context loading. It identifies repeated patterns across sessions, applies configurable lossless or lossy compression rules, and streams the most relevant compressed chunks back into models first for faster retrieval. It's designed for long-running chatbots, multi-agent simulations, and memory-limited embedding stores.<br/><br/>**Context Compressor Skill** [2] automates context management for long Clawdbot sessions by detecting when context limits approach, compressing old conversation history, and seamlessly transferring to fresh sessions. It features smart summarization that preserves critical details like decisions, code changes, and file states while removing filler content.<br/><br/>**Better Memory** [5] provides semantic memory with intelligent compression and context management. It stores local vector embeddings, performs semantic search via cosine similarity, auto-deduplicates entries, and applies priority-based compression as agents approach context limits while supporting identity persistence across sessions.<br/><br/>**Neural Memory** [4] offers associative memory with spreading activation for persistent, intelligent recall across sessions. It uses Hebbian learning, memory decay, contradiction detection, and temporal reasoning without requiring LLM dependency.<br/><br/>## Common Features<br/><br/>These skills share approaches to prevent context limit amnesia by compressing older information while preserving high-value context, enabling long-running agents to maintain continuity and decision history across multiple sessions.[1][2][5] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[session-handoff skill by softaworks/agent-toolkit](https://playbooks.com/skills/softaworks/agent-toolkit/session-handoff)<br/>4. [Tessl • The package manager for agent skills](https://tessl.io/registry/skills/github/softaworks/agent-toolkit/session-handoff)<br/>5. [creating-handoffs: Seamlessly transfer AI work sessions, never lose context \| skills.rest](https://skills.rest/skill/creating-handoffs)<br/><br/># Session Handoff: AI Agent Context Memory Transfer<br/><br/>**Session-handoff** is an agent skill that creates comprehensive handoff documents enabling AI agents to seamlessly continue work between sessions without losing context.[1][2]<br/><br/>## Problem Solved<br/>It addresses **context exhaustion** in long-running agent tasks by preserving session state, allowing fresh agents to continue work with "zero ambiguity."[1][2]<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/><br/>**Triggering conditions:**[2]<br/>- User requests to save state, create handoff, or pause work<br/>- Context window approaches capacity<br/>- Major task milestones completed<br/>- Work session ending<br/>- Proactively suggests after substantial work (5+ file edits, complex debugging, architecture decisions)<br/><br/>**Two main workflows:**[1]<br/><br/>**CREATE Workflow** - Saving context:<br/>1. Generate scaffold with `python scripts/create_handoff.py [task-slug]`<br/>2. Complete handoff document sections (Current State, Important Context, Next Steps, Decisions Made)<br/>3. Validate with `python scripts/validate_handoff.py` (checks for secrets, scores quality 0-100)<br/>4. Confirm handoff with user<br/><br/>**RESUME Workflow** - Loading context:<br/>1. List available handoffs<br/>2. Check staleness (verifies files, branches, commits since creation)<br/>3. Load handoff document completely<br/>4. Verify context before proceeding<br/><br/>## Context Chaining<br/>Supports linking handoffs to previous work via `--continues-from` flag, creating a lineage for multi-part tasks.[1][3]<br/><br/>## Installation<br/>Available via multiple package managers: `npx skills add`, `npx openskills install`, or `npx tessl i`[1][2] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[How to Build handoff Agent Skill \| SkillMD.ai](http://skillmd.ai/how-to-build/handoff-5/)<br/>4. [Agent Skills & Context - OpenHands Docs](https://docs.openhands.dev/sdk/guides/skill)<br/>5. [session-handoff skill by softaworks/agent-toolkit](https://playbooks.com/skills/softaworks/agent-toolkit/session-handoff)<br/><br/># Session Handoff Agent Skill Summary<br/><br/>The **session-handoff** skill is an agent tool that creates comprehensive handoff documents to enable seamless continuation of work across multiple AI agent sessions, solving context window exhaustion problems.[1][2]<br/><br/>## Key Functionality<br/><br/>**Triggering conditions:**[1]<br/>- User requests to save state/create handoff<br/>- Context window approaches capacity<br/>- Major task milestones completed<br/>- Work session ending<br/>- User says "pause," "save state," or "context is getting full"<br/><br/>**Proactive behavior:** Suggests handoffs after substantial work (5+ file edits, complex debugging, architecture decisions).[1][2]<br/><br/>## Core Workflows<br/><br/>**CREATE Workflow:**[2][5]<br/>1. Run `python scripts/create_handoff.py [task-slug]` to generate a pre-filled scaffold with timestamp, git branch, recent commits, and modified files<br/>2. Complete priority sections: Current State Summary, Important Context, Immediate Next Steps, Decisions Made<br/>3. Run `python scripts/validate_handoff.py <handoff-file>` to check completeness and detect secrets<br/>4. Confirm handoff with validation score and location<br/><br/>**RESUME Workflow:** Load previous handoff documents to continue with "zero ambiguity."[1][2]<br/><br/>## Installation<br/><br/>Install via CLI with `npx skills i softaworks/agent-toolkit/skills/session-handoff` or `npx tessl i github:softaworks/agent-toolkit --skill session-handoff`.[1][2]<br/><br/>The skill works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI and other AI coding assistants that support SKILL.md files.[1][3][4] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[Skills \| Cursor Docs](https://cursor.com/help/customization/skills)<br/><br/># Cursor Agent Skills, Obsidian, and Knowledge Graph Integration (2026)<br/><br/>## Agent Skills in Cursor<br/><br/>Cursor supports **Skills** as reusable markdown-based instructions for multi-step workflows.[5] Skills are created as `SKILL.md` files in `.cursor/skills/` directories and invoked with `/skill-name` commands. They differ from rules by being longer, detailed procedures for specific tasks rather than short guidelines.[5]<br/><br/>## Obsidian Agent Skills<br/><br/>Multiple Obsidian agent skills are available for 2026:[2][3][4]<br/><br/>- **obsidian-best-practices**: Covers plugin development, safety patterns, UI customization, CodeMirror 6, and CLI debugging workflows.[3]<br/>- **obsidian-cli**: Streamlines vault operations, task management, plugin development cycles, and live JavaScript inspection.[3]<br/>- These skills work across Cursor, Claude, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, and other AI coding assistants that support the Agent Skills standard.[3][4]<br/><br/>## Knowledge Graph and Semantic Memory<br/><br/>Rather than flat markdown files, agents now use **semantic graphs** for knowledge storage.[1] The AIngle protocol stores knowledge as semantic triples (subject-predicate-object) with cryptographic Proof-of-Logic (PoL) verification, enabling agents to verify claims without trusting each other.[1] This addresses compliance and KYC verification use cases where trustworthiness of assertions matters.[1]<br/><br/>## Sync and Integration<br/><br/>Obsidian skills enable vault automation through CLI integration, supporting note search, creation, property management, and link integrity maintenance across vaults.[4] Agent skills can be installed globally or per-project across multiple platforms.[4] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[obsidian-markdown - Agent Skill Guide - Agent Skills Guide](https://agentskills.guide/skills/pkarpovich-environment-obsidian-markdown)<br/><br/># Agent Skills, GitHub, and Knowledge Management in 2026<br/><br/>## Agent Skills Overview<br/><br/>Agent Skills is an open standard announced by Anthropic in December 2025 for packaging AI agent capabilities into portable, reusable modules.[1] The core of the system is the **SKILL.md file**, a Markdown document that defines what a skill does, how to use it, and execution rules.[1][2]<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/><br/>**Write Once, Works Everywhere**: A single skill directory works across 16+ major AI tools including Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, VS Code, and others.[1] This represents an industry-wide standardization adopted by Google, OpenAI, JetBrains, and GitHub within three months of the announcement.[1]<br/><br/>**Progressive Disclosure**: Agents only load skill names and descriptions initially, loading full content only when needed, allowing dozens or hundreds of skills without performance penalties.[1]<br/><br/>**Directory Structure**: Skills consist of a required SKILL.md file plus optional subdirectories for scripts, references, and assets.[1]<br/><br/>## Knowledge Management & Obsidian Integration<br/><br/>Several GitHub repositories now provide Agent Skills specifically for knowledge management tools like Obsidian:[3][5] These include skills for plugin development, vault management, CLI operations, and Obsidian-flavored Markdown syntax with wikilinks, callouts, and properties.[3][5]<br/><br/>## Broader Ecosystem<br/><br/>A growing ecosystem of Agent Skills repositories exists across platforms, with skills available for web browsing, ML tasks, product management, file-based planning, and other domains.[4] |

[SKILL.md: The Game-Changer Giving AI Agents Procedural Memory \| by Abhinav Dobhal \| Feb, 2026 \| Medium](https://medium.com/@abhinav.dobhal/skill-md-the-game-changer-giving-ai-agents-procedural-memory-035facf1e481)<br/>2. [Context Compression Strategies Skill \| Agent Skills](https://agent-skills.md/skills/muratcankoylan/Agent-Skills-for-Context-Engineering/context-compression)<br/>3. [muratcankoylan/Agent-Skills-for-Context-Engineering](https://github.com/muratcankoylan/Agent-Skills-for-Context-Engineering)<br/>4. [context-compression skill by muratcankoylan/agent-skills-for-context-engineering](https://playbooks.com/skills/muratcankoylan/agent-skills-for-context-engineering/context-compression)<br/>5. AgentSkillsRepo](https://agentskillsrepo.com/skill/muratcankoylan/agent-skills-for-context-engineering-context-compression)<br/><br/># SKILL.md and Agent Skills for Context Compression<br/><br/>**SKILL.md Framework (2026):**<br/>SKILL.md is a standardized markdown file format that packages domain expertise, workflows, and best practices into modular "skills" for AI agents.[1] It functions as a revolutionary framework that gives AI agents procedural memory, transforming them from autocomplete systems into reliable engineering colleagues with persistent context.[1] The format uses a three-tier loading mechanism optimizing token efficiency: Tier 1 loads metadata (30-100 tokens) at startup, Tier 2 loads instructions (<5,000 tokens) when tasks match, and Tier 3 loads scripts and templates only during active tool invocation.[1]<br/><br/>**Context Compression Strategies:**<br/>The Agent-Skills-for-Context-Engineering repository (13,711 stars) provides comprehensive skills for managing context in agent systems.[3] The context-compression skill addresses long-running sessions by optimizing "tokens-per-task" rather than tokens-per-request—the correct metric accounting for re-fetching costs when compression loses critical information.[2][4] <br/><br/>Three production-ready approaches exist:[2][4]<br/>- **Anchored Iterative Summarization**: Maintains structured summaries with explicit sections for session intent, file modifications, and decisions<br/>- **Opaque Compression**: Achieves 99%+ compression but sacrifices interpretability<br/>- **Regenerative Full Summary**: Produces readable output but may lose details across repeated cycles<br/><br/>The key insight is that structure forces preservation—dedicated sections act as checklists preventing silent information drift.[2][4] A critical weakness identified is "artifact trail integrity" (scoring 2.2-2.5/5.0), where agents struggle to track which files were created, modified, or deleted across long sessions.[4] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[Neural Memory Enhanced — AI Skill — Termo](https://termo.ai/skills/neural-memory-enhanced)<br/><br/># Neural Memory vs Better Memory Skills on ClawHub<br/><br/>## Neural Memory[1]<br/>- **Downloads:** 3.1K<br/>- **Stars:** 2<br/>- **Installs (all time):** 6<br/>- **Install command:** `clawdbot install nhadaututtheky/neural-memory`<br/>- **Features:** Associative memory with spreading activation, Hebbian learning, memory decay, contradiction detection, and temporal reasoning. Zero LLM dependency with neural graph architecture.<br/>- **Use cases:** Cross-session memory, persistent recall for facts/decisions/errors, causal chain tracing<br/><br/>## Better Memory[2]<br/>- **Features:** Semantic memory with vector embeddings, priority-based compression, identity persistence, token-budget-aware retrieval, auto-deduplication<br/>- **Install command:** `npx playbooks add skill openclaw/skills --skill better-memory`<br/>- **Use cases:** Preventing context-limit amnesia, semantic search over interactions, long-running user preferences, multi-session identity retention<br/><br/>## Key Differences<br/>Neural Memory uses graph-based associative recall through spreading activation, while Better Memory uses semantic embeddings for vector-based search. Neural Memory emphasizes causal relationships and contradiction detection; Better Memory prioritizes compression and token budgeting for context management. |

</details>
</tool-use>

It was created in December 2025 and has grown rapidly with 1,055 forks and 7 contributors.[2]<br/><br/>## Key Content<br/><br/>The repository is organized around three main skill categories:[2]<br/><br/>- **Foundational Skills**: Context fundamentals, degradation patterns, and compression strategies<br/>- **Architectural Skills**: Multi-agent patterns, memory systems, tool design, and hosted agents<br/>- **Operational Skills**: Context optimization and evaluation<br/><br/>## Recognition<br/><br/>The work has gained academic recognition—it's cited in peer-reviewed research from Peking University's State Key Laboratory of General Artificial Intelligence as foundational work on static skill architecture.[2]<br/><br/>## Latest Release<br/><br/>Version 1.1.0 (December 24, 2025) added LLM-as-a-Judge evaluation skills with TypeScript examples and comprehensive evaluation guidelines.[4]<br/><br/>The repository is ranked #3364 globally on GitHub among repositories with 8k+ stars.[1] |

# SK搜索报告：知识库管理 & 会话管理/上下文接续

---

## 类别A：知识库管理（Obsidian / PKM / 知识图谱）

### 1. kepano/obsidian-skills（官方级）

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| **名称** | obsidian-skills（含 obsidian-markdown, obsidian-bases, json-canvas, obsidian-cli） |
| **来源** | GitHub / skills.sh / Tessl |
| **描述** | Obsidian 官方开发者 kepano 出品，教 AI agent 操作 Obsidian Flavored Markdown（wikilinks、callouts、properties、embeds）、Bases 文件、JSON Canvas 可视化画布、CLI 交互。是 Agent Skills 生态中 Obsidian 类最权威的仓库 |
| **星数** | **13,163 stars** / 715 forks / 总安装 4,200+ |
| **最后更新** | 2026-03-02 |
| **安装方式** | `npx skills add kepano/obsidian-skills` 或 `npx tessl i github:kepano/obsidian-skills` |
| **URL** | https://github.com/kepano/obsidian-skills |

### 2. obsidian-ontology-sync（已安装）

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| **名称** | obsidian-ontology-sync v1.0.1 |
| **来源** | ClawHub / 本地已安装 |
| **描述** | 双向同步 Obsidian PKM（人类友好笔记）与结构化本体（机器可查询图谱）。自动从 Markdown 中提取实体和关系，维护本体图，并反馈改善笔记结构。支持 cron 定时同步 |
| **星数** | ClawHub 来源，openclaw/skills 仓库 1,086 stars |
| **最后更新** | 已安装版本 1.0.1 |
| **安装方式** | 已安装于 `C:\Users\乐迪\.agents\skills\obsidian-ontology-sync-1.0.1\` |
| **URL** | https://clawhub.ai/parthpandya1729/obsidian-ontology-sync |

### 3. obsidian-graph-query

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| **名称** | obsidian-graph-query |
| **来源** | GitHub / skills.sh |
| **描述** | 让 AI agent 直接查询 Obsidian vault 的知识图谱，使用图算法（BFS、最短路径、桥接检测、度分析）回答"哪些笔记链接最多？""哪些笔记是孤岛？"等问题 |
| **星数** | skills.sh 收录（88,000+ 技能生态） |
| **最后更新** | 活跃 |
| **安装方式** | `npx skills add azuma520/obsidian-graph-query` |
| **URL** | https://github.com/azuma520/obsidian-graph-query |

### 4. ontology（openclaw 通用本体管理）

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| **名称** | ontology |
| **来源** | ClawHub / playbooks.com |
| **描述** | 管理类型化知识图谱，支持创建、查询、链接实体和关系，用于 agent 规划。通用本体 skill，不限于 Obsidian |
| **星数** | openclaw/skills 仓库 1,086 stars |
| **最后更新** | 活跃 |
| **安装方式** | `npx playbooks add skill openclaw/skills --skill ontology` 或 `npx clawhub@latest install ontology` |
| **URL** | https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/ontology |

### 5. knowledge-management（ClawHub PKM）

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| **名称** | knowledge-management |
| **来源** | ClawHub / playbooks.com |
| **描述** | 将 agent 的知识条目自动分类（Research/Decision/Insight/Lesson/Pattern/Project/Reference/Tutorial），存为带时间戳的 Markdown 文件。支持 `km sync`/`km classify`/`km summarize`/`km cleanup` 命令。纯本地运行，无需 API |

| **URL** | https://playbooks.com/skills/openclaw/skills/knowledge-management |

| **来源** | ClawHub / playbooks.com |

## 类别B：会话管理 / 上下文接续

### 1. session-handoff（softaworks）

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| **名称** | session-handoff |
| **来源** | GitHub / skills.sh / Tessl / agentskills.me |
| **描述** | 解决上下文耗尽问题的核心 skill。创建全面的交接文档让新 agent 无缝继续工作。支持 CREATE（生成交接脚手架→填写状态/上下文/决策/下一步→验证检测密钥+质量评分）和 RESUME（列出交接→检测过期→加载→验证）两个流程。支持 `--continues-from` 链式交接。5+ 文件编辑或复杂调试后主动建议交接 |
| **星数** | skills.sh + 多平台收录 |
| **最后更新** | 活跃 |
| **安装方式** | `npx skills add softaworks/agent-toolkit/session-handoff` 或 `npx tessl i github:softaworks/agent-toolkit --skill session-handoff` |
| **URL** | https://github.com/softaworks/agent-toolkit (session-handoff) / https://agentskills.me/skill/session-handoff |

## 汇总与推荐优先级

| 类别 | 最推荐 | 理由 |
|------|--------|------|
| **知识库管理** | **kepano/obsidian-skills** + 已装的 **obsidian-ontology-sync** | 前者 13k stars、官方出品、覆盖 Markdown/Canvas/CLI；后者已安装且直接支持实体关系提取 |
| **知识库管理**（补充） | **obsidian-graph-query** | 图算法查询能力独特，补充本体同步的不足 |
| **会话管理** | **session-handoff** + **context-compression** | session-handoff 是标准交接方案；context-compression 来自 13.5k stars 仓库，学术级质量 |
| **会话管理**（补充） | **better-memory** | 语义搜索+优先级压缩+身份持久化，适合长期运行场景 |

已安装的 `obsidian-ontology-sync` 和 `memory` 已覆盖部分需求，建议重点评估 **session-handoff** 和 **context-compression** 填补跨窗口交接的空白。

---
